## [00:00 - 01:07]

This is the list of things you shouldn't do when it comes to eye contact. First thing you shouldn't do, don't dart. People feel nervous. They don't want to hold eye contact with people. And as a result, it's just looking at people really quickly. And it makes you seem a little bit shifty. Don't scan. They kind of look at the notes and then they go, good morning, good afternoon, good evening, everyone. It's great to see you all. Thank you so much for being a part of today's session. They're not looking at anyone. Don't look sequentially. Don't look to this side, then the center, then the other side. Oh, then obviously back to this side, then the center, then the other side. Because that sequence, once repeated over and over again, anything default becomes non-functional. I don't know where this piece of advice came from. But some people often say, oh, if you're nervous, don't worry. Just look over people's heads. No, if I just look slightly above the camera. So the camera's there. Imagine I looked here. How engaging would I be if I just kind of spoke to you like this for the entire sessions that we ran? Don't look at one single person for too long. Don't just come out and go, Hi, everyone. It's good to see you all here. Great to be in everybody's presence. And then you're just focusing on one person. That person's going to freak out. Simple list of things that you should not do.
